name: Marmot
what_it_does: Open-source data catalog that helps teams discover and manage data assets across an organization. Ships as a single binary and catalogs databases, APIs, message queues, and data pipelines with full-text search, interactive lineage graphs, and metadata management.
use_cases: Discovering data assets across an organization
Tracing data lineage and understanding impact of changes
Documenting data ownership and business context
Searching metadata across databases, APIs, queues, and pipelines
best_when: You need a lightweight, self-hosted data catalog without the complexity of enterprise platforms like DataHub or Amundsen. Good for small-to-medium teams wanting quick data discovery.
avoid_when: You need deep integrations with specific cloud data platforms, advanced data quality monitoring, or enterprise-scale governance features. No MCP server available.

auth.notes: Authentication details not documented in README. Self-hosted deployment.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: No MCP server. REST API exists but documentation is sparse in README -- details are on external docs site.
known_agent_gotchas: No MCP server -- would need custom integration via REST API or CLI
Documentation primarily on external site, not in README

security_score_components.security_notes: Community/specialized tool. Apply standard security practices for category. Review documentation for specific security requirements.
